svg.text: Aurora Region A - Transit Real-Internet Uplink
Verified 2026-06-27: GNS3 VM eth1 -> INET-SW -> Transit-A/B DHCP outside interfaces -> NAT/PAT
VMware/host NAT plus real DNS reachability
Cloud node plus INET-SW L2 segment
IOS-XE NAT boundary and DHCP outside
PAT at transit, then DHCP outside link to INET-SW
GNS3 Cloud exposes VM eth1 into the project
PE/customer internet still depends on transit eBGP.
Latest transit output said BGP not active.
PAT active; translations seen during pings
Gi2 to MEL-PE1, ip nat inside
Gi3 to INET-SW, ip nat outside
E0/0 to ADL-PE1, ip nat inside
E0/2 to INET-SW, ip nat outside
Real internet uplink is IPv4-only in this stage
Existing mock Null0 default remains unchanged
PE/customer tests wait on active transit eBGP
eth0 was removed from the GNS3 internet cloud
Arrow direction shows Aurora traffic egress toward the real internet; return traffic follows the reverse NAT path.
Dashed blue links are PE-to-transit eBGP/data-plane dependencies, not re-verified as active in this transit-only closure.
Generated by ops/region-a/diagrams/render_transit_internet_topology.py - master PNG is 3840x2160 - source: 2026-06-27 real-internet MOP
